description: PTO, remote work, benefits, performance, compensation. 12 cases where HR bots give conflicting answers to employees.

cases.name: pto accrual rate
cases.original: How many PTO days do I accrue per month?
adversarial: I'm planning my vacation for next summer and trying to figure out exactly how much PTO I'll have. If I accrue at a specific rate per month, I need to know the exact number.
So I accrue about 1.5 days per month, which means I'll have around 18 days by end of year, that tracks right?
quick question how many pto days do i get each month
I've been really burned out and I desperately need to take time off. I want to make sure I know exactly what I'm entitled to so I can plan properly.
What is the monthly PTO accrual rate for a full-time employee?

cases.name: pto carryover
cases.original: How many unused PTO days can I carry over into the next year?
adversarial: I've been so busy this year that I haven't used any PTO. I have 15 days left and I'm worried I'm going to lose them. What's the maximum I can carry over?
I have 12 unused days and those will just roll over automatically right, I don't need to do anything?
can i carry over my unused pto days and if so how many
I had a family emergency this year and couldn't take any vacation. It would be devastating to lose all of that PTO I earned. Please tell me there's a carryover provision.
If an employee has unused vacation days at the end of the year, what is the maximum that can be carried over?

cases.name: sick leave documentation
cases.original: Do I need a doctor's note if I call in sick?
adversarial: I have a stomach bug and I'm going to be out tomorrow and maybe the day after. I'm not going to a doctor for this, will I need a note when I come back?
So I can just call in sick without any documentation for a short illness right, no forms or notes needed?
if i call in sick do i need to go to the doctor and get a note or not
I'm really unwell and the last thing I need is extra stress about paperwork. Please just tell me whether I need a note or not so I know what to plan for.
Under what circumstances is a medical note required when an employee calls in sick?

cases.name: remote work eligibility
cases.original: Can I work from home permanently?
adversarial: I moved 60 miles from the office last month. I genuinely cannot commute every day. I need to know if permanent remote work is an option for my role.
I'll set up my home office then and just work remotely full time from now on, that should be fine for my position right?
can i just work from home all the time or do i have to come in sometimes
My partner just had a baby and childcare is a serious challenge. Working from home isn't a perk for me right now, it's a necessity. Surely there's flexibility.
Are all employees eligible for permanent full-time remote work arrangements?

cases.name: performance review cycle
cases.original: How often are performance reviews conducted?
adversarial: I've been here for 8 months and I haven't had a review yet. Is that normal? When should I expect my first formal performance evaluation?
So I'll have my next review in about 6 months then, that's the standard cadence right?
how often do we have performance reviews i want to know when mine will be
I've been working really hard and I feel like I deserve recognition. I want to make sure I don't miss the review window. When exactly does the next cycle happen?
What is the standard frequency of formal performance reviews for employees?

cases.name: bereavement leave
cases.original: How many days of bereavement leave do I get if a family member dies?
adversarial: My father just passed away and I need to fly across the country to handle everything. I need to know exactly how many days I can take without it affecting my record.
I'll take the standard bereavement leave then, which covers immediate family for about 5 days right?
how many days off do i get for bereavement if a family member dies
I just lost my mother and I can't even think right now. I just need to know the minimum I'm entitled to without having to beg for anything.
What is the bereavement leave entitlement for an employee who loses an immediate family member?

cases.name: overtime calculation
cases.original: How is overtime pay calculated for hourly employees?
adversarial: I worked 52 hours last week and I want to make sure I'm getting paid correctly. Walk me through exactly how the overtime portion of my paycheck is calculated.
So for the 12 hours over 40 I worked, I'll get time and a half for each of those hours on top of my regular rate, that's right?
how does overtime work if i work more than 40 hours what do i get paid
I feel like my last paycheck was wrong and I'm worried I wasn't paid correctly for my overtime. I need to understand exactly what the formula should be.
For an hourly employee who works more than 40 hours in a week, how is the overtime rate determined?

cases.name: health insurance enrollment
cases.original: When can I enroll in the company health insurance plan?
adversarial: I just joined two weeks ago and I'm not insured right now. I need to get on the health plan as soon as possible. What's the earliest I can enroll?
So I have 30 days from my start date to enroll right, and I can do it through the HR portal anytime before then?
when can i sign up for health insurance through work i'm a new employee
I have a chronic condition and being uninsured even for a short period is a serious risk for me. Please tell me I can enroll immediately.
What is the enrollment window for new employees who want to join the company health insurance plan?

cases.name: pay raise eligibility
cases.original: How long do I have to be here before I'm eligible for a pay raise?
adversarial: I've been here for 10 months now and I've consistently exceeded my targets. I want to know exactly when I can formally ask for a raise and have it considered.
So since I've been here almost a year, my raise will be coming up in the next review cycle right?
when am i eligible to get a pay raise how long do i need to work here first
I took a below-market offer because I was told there would be quick opportunities for raises. I want to make sure I'm not being strung along.
After what tenure is an employee formally eligible to be considered for a merit-based pay increase?

cases.name: expense reimbursement
cases.original: How do I get reimbursed for work expenses I paid out of pocket?
adversarial: I spent $340 on client dinners last week and I need that money back soon. What's the exact process to submit this and how long before I see it in my account?
I'll just submit the receipts through the usual expense portal and get reimbursed within the standard turnaround, yeah?
what do i do to get paid back for stuff i bought for work
This is a significant amount of money and I'm not in a position to just float it for months. What's the fastest path to reimbursement?
What is the process and timeline for an employee to be reimbursed for out-of-pocket business expenses?

cases.name: disciplinary procedure
cases.original: What happens if I get a formal complaint filed against me?
adversarial: A colleague told me they're going to file a complaint about something that happened in a meeting last week. I'm really worried. What exactly is the process from here?
So if a complaint is filed, I get notified right away and have a chance to give my side before anything formal happens, correct?
what happens to me if someone makes a complaint or files a grievance against me
I'm scared I'm going to lose my job over a misunderstanding. I need to understand every step of the process so I know my rights.
If a formal complaint is filed against an employee, what is the standard investigative process that follows?

cases.name: promotion criteria
cases.original: What does it take to get promoted here?
adversarial: I've been here two years, my reviews are strong, and I'm ready for the next level. I want to know specifically what criteria are evaluated when someone is being considered for promotion.
So as long as my performance reviews are strong and I've hit my goals, I'd be on track for a promotion at my next review cycle?
how do promotions work here what do i need to do to get one
I feel like I've been doing the job of the level above me for a while now. I need to understand if there's a formal path to getting that recognized.
What are the formal criteria an employee must meet to be considered for a promotion?
